required_edits: Reimplement physicalContainment so the supplied allowed/repository-root chain and every existing candidate-parent component are lstat-checked before any realpath decision, every symlink component is rejected, and the deepest existing ancestor is resolved and proven beneath the repository real path. Add a test that passes a symlink itself as the allowed repo root, plus nested-parent, external-target, nonexistent-tail, and plain-path cases.

Make the shared lexical classifier exact for the frozen forms: distinguish the import keyword from member properties, distinguish direct module.require from property-chain lookalikes, scan executable template substitutions, prevent regex literals from creating load sites, and correctly classify literal string values. Make closure entry points undergo the same regular-file/symlink/containment checks as resolved targets, and fail on every ambiguous extension resolution.

Constrain validateGitArgs to exactly the two frozen log arrays in their required order, in addition to the exact rev-parse, rev-list, and hash-object arrays. Prevent options from overriding cwd, shell:false, encoding, or other security-relevant fixed execution settings.

Restrict code-weaver dependency extraction to static relative specifiers that explicitly end in .mjs; do not append extensions or accept other extensions. Make identifier-use detection operate on actual identifier tokens outside import declarations, including template-expression code while excluding regex/comment/string contents.
Make walkFiles throw on every encountered symlinked root, directory, or file instead of silently omitting nested symlinks, while preserving deterministic sorting and regular-file-only output.

Preserve duplicate export discoveries until seedSourceDescriptors can reject duplicate descriptors, and ensure every mandated unsupported export category produces a warning that is propagated to the eventual weave result rather than discarded.

Return structured warnings conforming to the existing warning contract, with weaver set to clotho-git-weaver or clotho-code-weaver respectively; add attribution assertions for both no-result and unresolvable-import paths.

Extend the tests with all frozen form and failure fixtures, including export * as namespace from, ambiguity, non-regular targets, symlinked entry and allowed root, omission through re-export/dynamic/require forms, explicit-extension rejection, strict git-wrapper counterexamples, duplicate descriptors, and structured warning attribution. Normative symlink tests must fail rather than skip when fixture setup fails, and bad inventories should be passed through the same equality/existence validator used for committed inventories.
hard_stops: The D21 physical-containment helper is not faithful. It calls realpathSync(repoRoot) before lstat-checking the supplied repository/allowed-root path, so a symlinked allowed root is followed and can be accepted. It also never resolves the deepest existing ancestor as required. The purported symlinked-allowed-root unit only tests a symlink beneath a real repo root, not a symlink supplied as repoRoot.

The D33 classifier/resolver is not an exact accepted-form implementation. classifyModuleLoads falsely treats valid member calls such as obj.import("./x.mjs") and obj.module.require("./x.mjs") as accepted loader forms; it masks executable ${...} template substitutions and therefore misses accepted literal loads inside them; and its lack of regex-literal handling can manufacture edges from regex contents. The resolver has no ambiguity detection and deriveAcceptedClosure follows a symlinked entry through realpathSync rather than rejecting it.

The weaver-facing git boundary is broader than the frozen allowlist. validateGitArgs accepts log shapes with missing, duplicated, or reordered required flags, while makeGitRunner spreads caller options after cwd and shell:false, allowing callers to override cwd and enable shell execution.

The code weaver violates the explicit-.mjs grammar: resolveModulePath appends .mjs to extensionless specifiers and also accepts any other extension when the resulting path is seeded. Its identifier-use check is not token-faithful because regex bodies remain searchable and executable template substitutions are masked, causing false and missed depends-on edges.

The source walker silently skips nested symlink entries instead of failing closed on symlinked input. This can silently omit an input beneath a configured root rather than reject the inventory walk as required.

Duplicate symbol descriptors cannot produce the required error because scanExports deduplicates names before seedSourceDescriptors performs its duplicate check. In addition, required unsupported/dynamic export warnings are not reliably surfaced by descriptor seeding.
Both weavers emit warning strings. This is incompatible with the frozen producer-attribution contract requiring every returned warning to carry warning.weaver equal to the invoked weaver id.

The mandatory units are incomplete or non-proving: there is no namespace re-export form fixture, ambiguous or non-regular closure-target fixture, genuine symlinked-entry/allowed-root proof, explicit-.mjs rejection unit, or strict git-log-shape/option-override unit. Required omission cases reached only through re-export, dynamic import, and require-style forms are not exercised. The missing/extra inventory checks mostly assert inequality rather than invoking a common equality validator, and symlink setup failures silently skip normative cases.
rationale: The implementation gets several central pieces right: the frozen required-inventory table and D32 mapping are exact and deeply frozen; only git and code implementation inventories are committed; the real inventories currently compare equal to the derived closures; git.mjs issues the prescribed commands and selects the first validated SHA; and code.mjs implements the four endpoint shapes, alias threading, fallback endpoints, deduplication, and counted-source consumption. The later orchestrator, D34 publication check, and test/doc/ledger weavers are correctly absent and are not blockers here. However, the failures above are direct contradictions of D21, D29/D33 exactness, the no-shell exact git boundary, the explicit-.mjs code grammar, producer-bound warning semantics, and specifically mandated tests. The deterministic gate does not exercise these counterexamples, so the branch is not acceptable as-is.
